symptoms.symptom_name: Painless deep punched-out plantar ulcer over the first metatarsal head with heavy surrounding callus

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A painless, callus-rimmed, punched-out ulcer over a plantar pressure point is the classic neuropathic diabetic ulcer.
Arterial ulcers are painful, on toes/malleoli and lack heavy callus.

symptoms.symptom_name: Long-standing bilateral stocking-glove sensory loss (feet like blocks of wood)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symmetrical distal sensory loss is the hallmark of diabetic polyneuropathy and explains the painless ulcer.

symptoms.symptom_name: One week of polyuria, polydipsia and profound fatigue

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Osmotic symptoms with fatigue indicate significant hyperglycaemia and dehydration.
Osmotic diuresis with lethargy in T2DM is the prodrome of HHS.

symptoms.symptom_name: Foul odour and purulent yellow discharge from the wound

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Malodour and purulence indicate established wound infection, possibly with anaerobic/deep tissue involvement.

symptoms.symptom_name: Warm erythema and swelling spreading ~4cm proximally from the wound

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Spreading warm erythema marks invasive soft-tissue infection extending beyond the wound.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Poorly controlled type 2 diabetes / impending HHS
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severe hyperglycaemia on a patient-obtainable home glucometer — admissible in telehealth, unverified; drives urgency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound lethargy and flushing without reported fever

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Systemic upset with an infective source in a frail diabetic who may not mount fever — sepsis must be excluded.
Frothy urine suggests significant proteinuria consistent with diabetic kidney disease.
Progressive visual loss in long-standing diabetes suggests retinopathy; also the cause of the insulin-administration failure.

symptoms.symptom_name: Missed evening long-acting insulin (vision-related administration failure)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Omitted basal insulin is a direct, reversible driver of the severe hyperglycaemia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absent monofilament sensation to mid-calf bilaterally

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Loss of protective sensation confirmed on examination — a sealed clinician finding; the AI infers neuropathy from the history, not a monofilament test.
Weak pulses suggest some arterial compromise affecting healing — a sealed clinician examination finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Deep wound not confidently probing to bone on sterile swab

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Probe-to-bone assessment plus imaging is needed to exclude osteomyelitis — a sealed clinician manoeuvre unavailable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals (BP 145/85, HR 98, Temp 37.6, RR 18, SpO2 98%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Borderline low-grade temperature and mild tachycardia — sealed clinician measurement; the AI cannot obtain reliable vitals remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Denies fever, chills, chest pain, vomiting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of overt fever/rigors slightly lowers probability but does NOT exclude sepsis in a frail diabetic.
No vomiting or Kussmaul breathing makes DKA less likely than HHS, but bloods are still required.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Infected neuropathic foot with ascending cellulitis
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Infected neuropathic diabetic foot ulcer with cellulitis
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Painless callus-rimmed plantar ulcer + dense neuropathy + purulence + spreading erythema is a limb-threatening infected diabetic foot.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Poorly controlled T2DM / impending HHS with possible sepsis
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Osmotic symptoms + CBG 22.4 + missed insulin + lethargy signal metabolic emergency and immune compromise.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Venous blood glucose, ketones, serum osmolality and blood gas
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantify severity of hyperglycaemia and distinguish HHS from DKA — cannot be obtained via telehealth.
Assess for systemic infection/sepsis, renal function and baseline before antibiotics.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Wound swab for microscopy, culture and sensitivities
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Directs targeted antibiotic therapy for the infected ulcer.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Plain foot X-ray (and MRI if indicated) for osteomyelitis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Deep wound over bone — image to exclude osteomyelitis; cannot be done remotely.
Confirms chronic glycaemic control to guide longer-term diabetes management.
Weak pedal pulses — assess arterial supply relevant to healing and revascularisation.
